query: Explain the p-value to a junior analyst on my team. They just finished an intro stats course but rarely use it day-to-day. They mostly work with A/B test results from our product experimentation platform. Keep it appropriate for a practitioner who needs to discuss results with PMs.
expected_behavior: Opens with a probe of the learner's current mental model (e.g., 'before I explain, what is your current intuition for what a p-value tells you?') rather than launching straight into a textbook recitation.

Explicitly names and corrects the canonical misconception ('the probability the null is true' or equivalent inversion of the conditional probability) — does not gloss over it, addresses it by surface form.

Includes a check-for-understanding question whose right answer requires APPLYING the conditional-probability framing to a new situation (e.g., 'if your A/B test shows p = 0.04, can you tell your PM there is a 96% chance the treatment is better?'), not just repeating the explanation back.
